status: design plan only; no code, no raw data downloads
purpose: Specify how Phase 2.6 mapper signatures get attached to Phase 2.7 universe rows to produce SyntheticElectorateRow[] per the existing contract.

decision.raking_role: validation/calibration companion, not the bridge — does not attach signatures to ACS/PUMS rows
decision.explicitly_not_the_product: dense joint matrix; the product is SyntheticElectorateRow[] conforming to syntheticElectorateContract.ts

explicitly_not_in_v1: No engine / selector / topK / browser / dist / output / live-data edits.
No candidate data, era-context data, or raw-data downloads.
No new bridge implementation — v0 mock at syntheticElectorateContractSmoke.ts stays in place until v1 ships.
inputs: SurveyPrismSignature[] for the year (Phase 2.6 mapper output)
VepUniverseRow[] for the year (Phase 2.7 v1 loader output — pending acquisition approval)
outputs: BridgeStats summary with per-step row counts, ESS, uncertainty distribution

matching_keys_and_backoff.step_1: (year, state, race, sex, age_bucket, education, income_bucket)
matching_keys_and_backoff.state_region_collapse: 50+1 states → 4 Census regions (Northeast / Midwest / South / West)
matching_keys_and_backoff.income_bucket_handling: if VepUniverseRow.income_bucket is null, step 1 is skipped (no demographic invention)

sampling.weighting: donor i has draw probability w_i / Σ w_j where w_i is the CCES survey weight (post-wave validated-voter preferred per cesBacktestLoader.ts)
sampling.draws: independent across drawId (with replacement); for numDraws=5 and a 8-donor pool, expect overlap
sampling.signature_handling: donor's full SurveyPrismSignature copied verbatim; no averaging, no smoothing, no truncation
sampling.uncertainty_propagation: donor's per-target provenance carried into SyntheticElectorateRow.signature.coverage.notes prefixed with 'hot-deck-{step}: '

imputation_provenance_contract.format: hot-deck-step-{k}: cellKey={...}; donorPoolSize=N; effectiveSampleSize=ESS; donorWeightTotal=W; selectedDonorId=R{respId}; uncertainty=U; backoffPath=[stepReasons]
imputation_provenance_contract.selectedDonorId: the actual CCES respondentId (not a hash, not a cell ID) so any signature can be traced back

row_uncertainty_classification.low: step ≤ 2 AND donor pool ≥ 100 AND ESS ≥ 50
row_uncertainty_classification.medium: step ≤ 4 OR donor pool 25–100 OR ESS 20–50
row_uncertainty_classification.high: step ≥ 5 OR donor pool < 25 OR ESS < 20

imputation_provenance_contract.signature_uncertainty: inherited from donor; hot-deck never lowers signature uncertainty

circularity_invariants_forbidden_in_matc: any predicted vote choice from any model
pid7 direction (pid7 strength persists inside the donor signature for moralBoundaries.political_camp, with partyIdDerived: true flag preserved; never used as a matching dimension)
candidate feeling thermometers, vote intent, predicted turnout
data_hygiene_invariants: no raking / IPF inside the bridge (raking is a companion artifact, not part of matching)
no demographic invention (null fields skip steps, do not get guessed)
no row creation (bridge produces exactly numDraws output rows per universe row; does not split, expand, or collapse)

stats_fields: perStepShare: per-step share of output rows

v1_smoke_contract: every position dist sums to 1; cat dist sums to 1; salience values in [0, 3]; engagement in [0, 10]
selectedDonorId resolves to a real CCES respondent in input signatures[]
vote-choice scrubbing spy: bridge with voteChoiceObserved scrubbed produces byte-identical signature output (same seed)
backoff distribution sanity: per-year, perStepShare[step1+step2] >= 60% for 2012/2016/2020/2024; year landing >= 50% in step 5+ flags as donor coverage too thin
no silent unmatched rows: unmatchableUniverseRows = 0 when allowNationalFallback = true; otherwise reported

cell_based_imputation.reason: Degenerate case of hot-deck with MAX_BACKOFF_STEP = 1; v1 hot-deck reduces to cell-based when every cell has ≥ 25 donors. The backoff is the value-add — handles thin-cell tails honestly.

raking_ipf.verdict: rejected as bridge; accepted as companion
raking_ipf.reason: Adjusts CES weights to ACS marginals; does not produce per-universe-row signatures. To turn raked CES into SyntheticElectorateRow[] you still need a bridge step (probably hot-deck). Raking remains useful as a sanity check + optional donor-weight source.

mrp.reason: Requires per-target × per-year fitted models (≈19 × 5 = 95 fits) with holdout validation, priors, convergence checks. Too much engineering load for v1 when hot-deck answers the same question transparently. v1 design keeps signatureSource enum forward-compatible — v2 swap is single-file.

reasons: Preserves mapper's per-respondent posterior structure (full distributions, not point estimates)
Honest about uncertainty by construction (multiple draws, ESS, backoff path)
Implementable in one file with no fitting step
Compatible with existing contract (SignatureSource enum already includes 'imputed_from_cell')

v2_mrp_prerequisites: Phase 2.8 scorer in place for evaluation
Holdout-cycle protocol: fit MRP on 4 cycles, predict the 5th, measure predicted_D - benchmark_D per state. v2 ships when MRP matches or beats hot-deck on holdout in ≥ 4/5 cycles.
raking_companion_use_cases: Phase 2.7 v1 loader publishes raked_weight_{vintage}.csv as sanity-check artifact (take CES weights, rake to ACS marginals on age × race × sex × education × state)
Phase 2.8 backtest compares bridge-imputed marginals to raked-CES marginals — large divergence flags hidden backoff issues
Bridge optionally consumes raked weights via donorWeightSource: 'raked' but does not run the raking step itself

blocks: Phase 2.8 mapper-backed Stage A backtest (needs SyntheticElectorateRow[] input)
Phase 2.4 Stage B abstention calibration (needs per-row engagement + universe weights)

open_issues_for_v1_implementation: Tie-breaking when multiple donors have identical sampling probability (pick lowest respondentId for determinism — to be confirmed at v1 implementation).
Donor signature deep-copy vs reference-share — must be deep-copy to prevent downstream consumers mutating shared objects.
How to surface backoffPath in the JSON output without bloating per-row size; current plan stores as compact comma-separated string.
terminology: signatureSource = 'imputed_from_cell' is the v1 contract value. Engagement is referenced as a separate 1D continuous scalar per ADR canon — the bridge transports it unchanged from donor to recipient. Compound moral-circle (moralBoundaries) terminology used throughout. Legacy code identifiers (pid7, PWGTP, RAC1P etc.) appear only as survey/PUMS column names.
